architecture.summary: Knowledge Capsules are structured nonparametric memory units that represent normalized relational knowledge, constructed from document corpora using a frozen base model. An External Key-Value Injection (KVI) framework compiles capsules into attention-compatible key-value representations that are injected directly into the model's attention computation, allowing external knowledge to participate at the memory level rather than as context tokens. The frozen base model processes queries, and the injected KV pairs from capsules augment the attention mechanism without parameter updates.

biblio.title: Knowledge Capsules: Structured Nonparametric Memory Units for LLMs

claims.statement: Knowledge Capsules consistently outperform RAG and GraphRAG across multiple QA benchmarks, with improved stability and accuracy in long context and multi-hop reasoning scenarios
Knowledge Capsules require no parameter updates to the base model

RAG operates purely through context expansion, where external knowledge competes as tokens within the attention mechanism, making its influence indirect and often unstable in long context and multi-hop reasoning scenarios
Knowledge Capsules can be constructed directly from document corpora using a frozen base model

The External Key-Value Injection framework enables external knowledge to directly participate in the model's attention computation by compiling capsules into attention-compatible key-value representations
